title: Koshi Province - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Woman-police scuffle video goes viral: what really happened in Sunsari eviction? By Arjun Subedi Footage showing police holding a woman by the neck during a demolition drive raises questions over what led to the confrontation. Road accidents rise sharply in Koshi Province, exposing coordination gaps By Arjun Subedi Deaths and injuries increase over three fiscal years as poor enforcement and weak i
content:
Woman-police scuffle video goes viral: what really happened in Sunsari eviction? By Arjun Subedi Footage showing police holding a woman by the neck during a demolition drive raises questions over what led to the confrontation. Road accidents rise sharply in Koshi Province, exposing coordination gaps By Arjun Subedi Deaths and injuries increase over three fiscal years as poor enforcement and weak inter-agency coordination undermine road safety. Women in Jhapa’s Bahundangi turn elephant fear into opportunity through community homestay By Parbat Portel Local women are running an ‘elephant-friendly’ homestay that provides income, strengthens community ties and redefines the village’s identity. Solar lights along Bhedetar–Sindhuwa section of Koshi Highway lie defunct By Binod Ghimire Damaged warning systems and lack of maintenance raise safety concerns as drivers report higher accident risks in fog-prone stretches. Flood damages under-construction bridges in Panchthar and Taplejung By Laxmi Gautam Structures on Mewa and Naruwa rivers suffer major setbacks. Dharan deploys city police to protect drinking water catchment By Binod Ghimire Municipal officials keep a 12-hour daily watch in Sardu and Nishane areas to prevent pollution and encroachment in key water sources. Harka Sampang wins Sunsari-1 By Post Report The Shram Sanskriti Party chair defeats Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Goma Tamang. RSP’s Bishwaraj Pokharel wins Okhaldhunga by five votes By Post Report Former AIG defeats Nepali Congress candidate Kumar Luitel in a razor-thin contest. RSP’s Surya Tamang wins Udayapur-2 By Post Report Defeats UML’s Ambar Rayamajhi by 7,154 votes. RSP’s Surya Lama takes lead in Udayapur-2 By Post Report Lama moves ahead with 4,287 votes as UML’s Rayamajhi trails. Shram Sanskriti Party chair Harka Sampang edges ahead in Sunsari-1 By Post Report RSP’s Goma Tamang trails as NCP’s Tikaram Rai remains third. RSP’s Rubina Acharya wins Morang-6 by wide margin By Post Report Congress veteran Shekhar Koirala finishes second with 12,850 votes RSP’s Balen Shah widens lead over Oli in Jhapa-5 By Post Report Shah secures 28,152 votes as UML chair KP Sharma Oli trails by a wide margin. UML candidate Suhang Nembang leads early in Ilam-2 By Post Report Shram Sanskriti Party’s Rai close behind as initial vote count shows tight race. Balen Shah widens lead over KP Sharma Oli in Jhapa-5 By Post Report RSP senior leader ahead by more than 6,000 votes as counting continues. Former deputy speaker Rana takes lead over ex-speaker Ghimire By Post Report Indira Rana ahead with nearly 3,000 votes in early count. UML secretary Bhanubhakta Dhakal leads in Tehrathum By Post Report Dhakal ahead of Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Surendra Karki in early count. Balen Shah takes commanding early lead in Jhapa-5 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party senior leader ahead of CPN-UML’s KP Sharma Oli in preliminary count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Balen Shah leads in Jhapa-5 By Post Report Shah ahead of CPN-UML’s KP Sharma Oli in early vote count. Kimathanka residents complain of neglect ahead of elections By Dipendra Shakya The remote settlement in northeastern Nepal remains cut off from the rest of the country, lacks reliable electricity, and depends on Tibet for daily necessities.
